exemplar.title: How does The Last Days of Judas Iscariot use the tension between divine and human mercy to explore the paradoxes of forgiveness in the path to self-redemption while challenging hypocrisy in the institutionalised Church?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does The Last Days of Judas Iscariot use the tension between divine and human mercy to explore the paradoxes of forgiveness in the path to self-redemption while challenging hypocrisy in the institutionalised Church?

subject.title: English A Lang & Lit
subject.description:
**IB English A Language & Literature** is a **Group 1** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **the study of language and literature through a wide variety of texts and media from different periods, styles, and cultures**, with a strong focus on **critical analysis of both literary and non-literary texts, examining how language choices create meaning and effect in different contexts**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Text analysis | 1h 15m | 2h 15m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL) |
| **Paper 2** | Comparative essay | 1h 45m | 1h 45m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL)  |
| **HL Essay** | Written assignment | N/A | 1200-1500 words | 25% (HL only) |
| **Individual Oral** | Oral presentation | 15 minutes | 15 minutes | 30% (SL), 20% (HL) |

strengths: The research question is explicitly stated, sharply focused and sustained throughout the investigation
The topic is communicated accurately and effectively, with clear purpose and sign-posted structure
Methodology demonstrates an appropriate range of relevant sources and methods, all clearly aligned with the research question
weaknesses: The methodology lacks a fully explicit justification for the selection and credibility of certain secondary sources
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay exhibits an exceptionally clear and sustained focus, with every section mapped directly to the research question and key concepts. Although there is a minor methodological omissionâno explicit criteria for source selectionâthe overall coherence, precision of purpose, and effective integration of methods place this criterion firmly at the top of the 5â6 band, meriting the full 6 marks.

strengths: Demonstrates a strong, coherent understanding of both the primary text and relevant secondary scholarship
Uses subject-specific terminology accurately and consistently to support the analysis
Integrates quotations and contextual knowledge effectively to develop thematic insights
weaknesses: Occasional colloquial language and minor misspellings interrupt the academic tone
A small number of secondary sources lack full academic rigour or clear verification
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student displays clear and substantial knowledge of the topic and sources, with accurate and consistent use of terminology. Minor lapses in formality and occasional questionable citations prevent this from reaching the very highest band, but the overall command of subject-specific concepts and effective source application justify 5 out of 6.

strengths: Research is consistently relevant and drawn from a wide range of primary and secondary texts, scholarly articles and theological writings
Analysis is insightful, with well-supported interpretations linking dramatic elements to theological concepts
The argument is focused, synthesising evidence into a coherent critical evaluation of mercyâs paradox and institutional hypocrisy
weaknesses: Evaluation of counter-perspectives is limited, and some analytical claims could probe deeper into underlying dramatic functions
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay demonstrates an excellent quality of research and depth of analysis, with well-supported conclusions and a focused, critical argument. Only minor gaps in counter-argument evaluation prevent a perfect score, placing the work at the top end of the 10â12 band with 11 out of 12 marks.

strengths: All formal presentation requirements (word count, title page, page numbering, MLA-style citations) are met consistently
Structure is clear and appropriate, with coherent section headings and logical progression
Layout, formatting and referencing conventions are applied correctly throughout
weaknesses: A few typographical errors and inconsistent italicisation of titles slightly detract from overall polish
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation is exemplary in structure, formatting and adherence to formal conventions, meeting all criteria for the top band. Minor typographical inconsistencies do not significantly impede readability or professionalism, justifying the full 4 marks.

strandSelections.reasoning:
**Correct application of formal presentation guidelines.** The student provides a word count, title, page numbers and a clearly formatted works-cited list in MLA-style layout. Headings are styled consistently, margins and line-spacing facilitate readability, and citations are embedded within the text.  

**Accuracy and consistency.** While a few bibliographic entries appear incomplete or potentially non-academic, the overall formatting adheres to standard conventions. Page references are supplied for most primary quotations.  

**Minor lapses.** Occasional typographical errors (e.g., spelling of âGuigisâ) and inconsistent italicisation of play titles are present but do not impede comprehension. Given that layout expectations are largely met, the strand merits a top-band (3â4) score of 4.

**Breadth and relevance of research.** The bibliography lists a range of primary and secondary sources: the play text, the Bible, papal encyclicals, journal articles and books on theology and criticism. These are consistently linked to the research question, demonstrating a clear attempt to ground claims in external scholarship.  

**Integration into argument.** Most secondary references are woven into the discussion to corroborate readings (e.g., Keller on divine mercy, Pope Francis on forgiveness), showing that the student selects materials for their pertinence to the line of inquiry. Direct quotations from both primary and secondary texts are generally apt and contextualised.  

**Minor weaknesses.** The credibility of a few sources (e.g., web article by âChristian Libraryâ) is not fully justified, and citation accuracy is sometimes shaky. Even so, the preponderance of relevant, scholarly material and its purposeful use align with the 7â9 band for research quality and relevance.

**Coherent line of argument.** The paper is logically organised from introduction through thematic sections to a reflective conclusion, showing clear attempts to build and synthesise an argument about mercy, self-forgiveness and Church hypocrisy.  

**Evidence of evaluation.** The student occasionally weighs contrasting viewpointsâe.g., juxtaposing Satanâs cynicism against Saint Monicaâs compassionâand comments on the implications of unresolved paradoxes. There is some reflection on the limitations of institutional frameworks.  

**Insufficient critical edge.** Evaluation often stops at agreement with secondary sources, and counter-arguments are not deeply explored. Some conclusions feel asserted rather than critically justified. This blend of strengths and shortcomings aligns with the middle (4â6) band, warranting a score of 5.

**Substantive but uneven analysis.** The student explores key scenes and character dynamics (e.g., Jesusâs unconditional statements, Satanâs denial of mercy) and connects them to theological perspectives, producing a coherent argument about paradoxes of forgiveness. Evidence is usually quoted and explained, indicating support for conclusions.  

**Areas of limitation.** Some analytical claims remain descriptive rather than probingâfor instance, recounting Mother Teresaâs hypocrisy without fully unpacking its dramatic function. Transitions between theological commentary and literary close-reading are sometimes asserted rather than argued, leading to occasional superficiality.  

**Overall assessment.** The essay moves beyond summary to offer thoughtful interpretation and some synthesis, but the depth and rigour are inconsistent, meriting a mid-band (4â6) score of 5.

**Meets structural requirements.** The essay contains a clear title page with word count, a table of contents through section headings (Introduction, thematic sections, Conclusion, Works Cited), and employs logical sub-headings that guide the reader. Paragraphing is coherent and sequencing supports the argument.  

**Cohesion and clarity.** Transitions between sections are generally smooth, and the progression from broad context to focused analysis to conclusion is appropriate for an EE.  

**Minor issues.** Occasional digressions and uneven section lengths affect balance, but all required components (abstract no longer compulsory) are present. The overall appropriateness of structure fulfils the highest band (3â4), so a score of 4 is awarded.

**Demonstrates solid subject knowledge.** The student shows a good grasp of Guirgisâs play, accurately summarising plot points, character functions (e.g., Satan as foil, Jesus as embodiment of mercy) and thematic strands. Connections to biblical passages and modern theological discourse evidence awareness of broader contexts.  

**Use of sources is competent but uneven.** Quotations from the primary text are generally apt, and secondary literature is invoked to bolster claims. Nevertheless, some citations are cursory, occasionally unverifiable, or lack depth of engagement, suggesting partial rather than fully effective application.  

**Overall judgement.** The essay displays clear understanding and generally effectiveâthough not consistently sophisticatedâuse of sources, placing it in the 3â4 band for knowledge and understanding.

**Mostly accurate terminology.** Key literary and theological termsââfoil,â âmetaphorical,â âdogma,â âinstitutional hypocrisy,â âredemptionââare employed correctly and help to articulate the argument.  

**Consistency with occasional lapses.** While the vocabulary is generally precise, moments of colloquial phrasing (âdownright comedic,â âshallow moral critiqueâ) and minor misspellings (âGuigisâ instead of âGuirgisâ) interrupt academic tone. Concepts such as âpurgatoryâ and âdivine mercyâ are invoked but not always rigorously defined.  

**Appropriate but not exemplary.** The command of subject-specific language supports analysis but lacks the polish and sustained precision characteristic of the top band, situating performance solidly within the 3â4 range.

**Research question is explicit and tightly framed.** It is presented verbatim at the start and focuses on one play, one central tension (divine vs. human mercy) and two clear purposes (exploring paradoxes of forgiveness and exposing Church hypocrisy). This scope is appropriate for the EE word limit and the languageâliterature discipline.  

**Logical integration with discussion.** Each section of the essay is sign-posted to correspond with a component of the question; analysis of characters (Jesus, Judas, Satan) feeds directly into exploring mercy, while later sections link that exploration to institutional hypocrisy. The line of argument never strays far from the RQ, indicating strong alignment.  

**Sustained focus.** Although the essay occasionally adds broad contemporary comments, these are used to illuminate the RQ rather than distract from it. The sustained, purposeful connection places this strand firmly in the 5â6 band.

annotations.comment: The justification for selecting this play demonstrates a clear understanding of its theological significance and relevance to the research question.
Integration of Mary Magdaleneâs testimony with secondary theological sources shows coherent knowledge and effective use of sources.
The well-placed section header maintains focus on the research question and improves navigability.
The foil between Satan and Jesus is well developed, employing quotations to support a nuanced interpretation of mercyâs paradox.
The inclusion of an accurate word count meets formatting criteria and demonstrates attention to submission requirements.
The introduction establishes a clear purpose and outlines the key concepts, setting a strong foundation for the essay.
The methodological approach is described but lacks justification for source selection criteria; clarify how and why specific sources were chosen.
The discussion of human mercy is largely descriptive; to strengthen analysis, probe deeper into the reasons behind these biases and their dramatic function.
The example of Mother Teresaâs hypocrisy is compelling and vividly illustrates institutional critique with precise textual support.
The research question is explicitly and effectively stated, providing a precise focus that guides the entire discussion.
The opening uses engaging language but includes colloquial phrases that detract from an academic tone; consider refining terminology for greater formality.
The interdisciplinary methodology is outlined but would benefit from explicit explanation of the criteria used to assess source credibility.
The clear section heading effectively signals the shift to thematic analysis, enhancing the essayâs overall structure.
The analysis of Jesusâs dialogue is insightful, effectively linking the playâs portrayal to biblical references and deepening the argument.
The title page clearly states the investigationâs title, fulfilling the formatting requirement and providing an immediate sense of focus.
Theological scholarship (Keller) is aptly integrated, reinforcing the literary analysis with relevant external research.
The introduction of the paradoxes section is clearly signposted, aiding the essayâs logical progression.
Consistent misspelling of the playwrightâs name (âGuigisâ) undermines academic precision; ensure correct terminology and spelling.
The invitation to audience reflection is noted, but the argument would benefit from evaluating potential counter-perspectives on these unresolved paradoxes.
The clear section header âHypocrisy in the institutionalised Churchâ effectively frames the forthcoming critique.
The analysis of Caiaphasâs political motivations displays sound subject knowledge and situates his actions within broader institutional critique.
The synthesis of institutional hypocrisy into a concise message effectively crystallizes the playâs central critique.
The section header linking Christian dogma to divine mercy supports the essayâs structured argument flow.
The portrayal of Jesusâs forgiveness, supported by vivid quotations, demonstrates coherent knowledge and insight into divine mercy.
The intersection of self-redemption and institutional critique is noted but lacks deeper evaluation of its thematic significance.
The conclusionâs synthesis of themes is evaluative and demonstrates excellent critical thinking.
The clear labelling of the conclusion enhances coherence and signals the essayâs closure effectively.
The source by âDoe, Johnâ appears to be non-academic; verify the credibility and relevance of each bibliography entry.
The web source citation lacks a publication date and standard formatting; add missing details to meet bibliographic conventions.
